authors: Slavin KV, North RB, Deer TR, Staats P, Davis K, Diaz R
title: Tonic and burst spinal cord stimulation waveforms for chronic, intractable pain: study protocol for a randomized controlled trial
study_question: To establish noninferiority of burst stimulation compared to tonic for pain intensity after 3 months.
population_assessed: Candidates for neurostimulation with chronic neuropathic pain of trunk/limbs, successful tonic trial.
follow_up_duration: 24 weeks controlled phase, then 6 months for up to 2 years open-label.
outcome_measures: Overall VAS score (primary), responder rate, paresthesia, pain relief, adverse events, quality of life.
inclusion_criteria: Informed consent, ≥22 years, chronic intractable neuropathic pain, baseline VAS ≥60, failed ≥3 treatments.
